title: Rhaphiolepis poilanei
intro: Rhaphiolepis poilanei, also known as the Poilane's hawthorn, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine to treat various health issues, including hypertension and cardiovascular diseases, due to its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular function. The bioactive constituents of Rhaphiolepis poilanei include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Rhaphiolepis poilanei is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are commonly used to treat cardiovascular and circulatory disorders.
